 Internet Traffic Decoder (NFAT).
 See http://www.xplico.org for more information.
 
 Copyright 2007-2019 Gianluca Costa & Andrea de Franceschi and contributors.
 This is free software; see the source for copying conditions. There is NO
 warranty; not even for MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.

 usage: mfbc [-h] [-s] [-l] [-i] [-c <config_file>] -p <port>
 	-c config file
 	-s silent
 	-p connection port
 	-i info (PEI generated by this manipulator)
 	-l print all log in the screen
 	-h this help
 	NOTE: parameters MUST respect this order!

commands.name: xplico

arguments.metavar: print all log in the screen
arguments.raw: -l print all log in the screen

arguments.metavar: print every second the deconding status
arguments.raw: -s print every second the deconding status

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# xplico -h
 xplico v1.2.2
 Internet Traffic Decoder (NFAT).
 See http://www.xplico.org for more information.
 
 Copyright 2007-2019 Gianluca Costa & Andrea de Franceschi and contributors.
 This is free software; see the source for copying conditions. There is NO
 warranty; not even for MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.
 
 This product includes GeoLite data created by MaxMind, available from http://www.maxmind.com/.
 
 usage: xplico [-v] [-c <config_file>] [-h] [-s] [-g] [-l] [-i <prot>] -m <capute_module>
 	-v version
 	-c config file
 	-h this help
 	-i info of protocol 'prot' 
 	-g display graph-tree of protocols
 	-l print all log in the screen
 	-s print every second the deconding status
 	-m capture type module
 	NOTE: parameters MUST respect this order!

apt_metadata.depends: apache2, binfmt-support, kali-defaults (>= 2026.2.1), lame, libapache2-mod-php, openssl, php-cli, php-json, php-sqlite3, python3, python3-httplib2, python3-psycopg2, recode, sox, sqlite3, tshark, libc6 (>= 2.38), libjson-c5 (>= 0.15), libmariadb3 (>= 3.0.0), libmaxminddb0 (>= 1.0.2), libndpi4.2t64 (>= 1.7), libpcap0.8t64 (>= 0.9.8), libpq5, libsqlite3-0 (>= 3.5.9), libssl3t64 (>= 3.0.0), zlib1g (>= 1:1.1.4), php-common
apt_metadata.description:
Network Forensic Analysis Tool (NFAT)
The goal of Xplico is extract from an internet traffic
capture the applications data contained. For example,
from a pcap file Xplico extracts each email (POP, IMAP,
and SMTP protocols), all HTTP contents, each VoIP call (SIP, MGCP, H323),
FTP, TFTP, and so on. Xplico is not a network protocol analyzer.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use mfbc because ### xplico.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `mfbc [-h] [-s] [-l] [-i] [-c <config_file>] -p <port>` as documented upstream for mfbc.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair mfbc with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `mfbc --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

arguments.name: -h
arguments.description: Option present in package usage line for mfbc.

arguments.name: -s
arguments.description: Option present in package usage line for mfbc.

arguments.name: -l
arguments.description: Option present in package usage line for mfbc.

arguments.name: -i
arguments.description: Option present in package usage line for mfbc.

arguments.name: -c
arguments.description: Option present in package usage line for mfbc.

arguments.name: -p
arguments.description: Option present in package usage line for mfbc.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install xplico. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `mfbc [-h] [-s] [-l] [-i] [-c <config_file>] -p <port>`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### xplico.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install xplico
